translation.en: I am not going because it is raining.

notes_md:
**bởi vì** means **because** — it gives the reason for something.

It’s a linking phrase (conjunction) that connects two clauses: one result, one reason. It usually comes in the middle of the sentence, but it can also start a sentence for emphasis.


In everyday speech, people often shorten it to **vì**, which means the same thing and sounds more natural. You can remember: **bởi vì** = a slightly more formal “because.”
exampleSentencesTranslated: I didn't go to school today because I am sick.
I bought flowers for you because today is your birthday.
I am staying home this weekend because I want to rest more.

translation.en: I am not going because it is raining.

content.sentence: Tôi không đi vì ___ trời mưa.

definitions.en: A word used to indicate the cause or reason for something.
definitions.vi: Từ dùng để chỉ nguyên nhân hoặc lý do của một sự việc.

example.en: I didn't go to school today because I was sick.
example.vi: Tôi không đi học hôm nay bởi vì tôi bị ốm.

notes:
bởi vì means because — it gives the reason for something.

It’s a linking phrase (conjunction) that connects two clauses: one result, one reason. It usually comes in the middle of the sentence, but it can also start a sentence for emphasis.

In everyday speech, people often shorten it to vì, which means the same thing and sounds more natural. You can remember: bởi vì = a slightly more formal “because.”
exampleSentences: Em không đi học hôm nay bởi vì em bị ốm.
Anh mua hoa cho chị bởi vì hôm nay là sinh nhật của chị.
Chị ở nhà cuối tuần này bởi vì muốn nghỉ ngơi nhiều hơn.
